(sha=d9cdbfb46db6705bc1b7e7de5cd0ecf6cd0f7ca7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Zalo inbound photo detection, carries `media` on `ZaloInboundMessage`, downloads detected photos with `saveRemoteMedia`, and passes media facts into the channel turn context.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection for the current capability gap: current main flattens zca-js attachment objects to text and does not pass Zalo media facts into `buildContext`. A real live Zalo reproduction of the proposed native-vision path is not established.
commands.review_followup: This draft PR should wait for contributor live proof plus maintainer review of the remote-fetch safety changes; ClawSweeper should not open a repair branch while the target live behavior is still unproven.

target.title: feat(zalouser): surface inbound photo attachments via media array
target.body:
## Summary

zca-js already delivers photo content via `TAttachmentContent.href` (see [`src/models/Message.ts`](https://github.com/RFS-ADRENO/zca-js/blob/main/src/models/Message.ts) in RFS-ADRENO/zca-js), but the zalouser plugin layer discards it - `normalizeMessageContent` flattens the object to a JSON-string fallback, so the agent only sees the photo URL embedded as text.

This change wires inbound photos into the same channel media pipeline Telegram and Discord extensions already use (`saveRemoteMedia` -> `toInboundMediaFacts` -> `media: [...]` array on `buildContext`). The goal is for inbound Zalo photos to arrive at the LLM as a native vision content block in the user turn, identical to how Telegram and Discord photo messages present today.

## What changes

- **New type** `ZaloInboundMedia` (`{kind: "image", url, thumbUrl?}`) in `types.ts`.
- **New helper** `extractInboundMedia` in `zalo-js.ts` detects photo content via:
  - `content.type === "photo"` (explicit), OR
  - image file extension in href (`.jpg/.jpeg/.png/.gif/.webp/.bmp`, case-insensitive, query-string-tolerant), OR
  - presence of a `thumb` URL (zca-js only ships `thumb` on photos).

  Returns `null` for text, link previews, files, stickers, audio - those continue through the existing `normalizeMessageContent` text path unchanged.
- `toInboundMessage` attaches the resolved media to the returned `ZaloInboundMessage`.
- `monitor.ts processMessage`:
  - Downloads the photo via `saveRemoteMedia` (mirrors `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-media.ts` pattern).
  - Passes the saved file as a top-level `media: [...]` array to `buildContext` via `toInboundMediaFacts`.
  - Relaxed early-return: photo-only messages (no text caption) are now processed; previous code returned early when `rawBody` was empty, silently dropping photo-only inbound messages.

## Tests

9 unit tests in `zalo-js.media.test.ts` for `extractInboundMedia` covering:
- Photo detection paths (explicit type, extension fallback, thumb fallback)
- Non-photo content shapes (text, link preview, sticker, audio, PDF) - confirm they return `null`
- Whitespace trimming on URLs
- Case-insensitive extension matching with query strings

## Real behavior proof

**Honest status (2026-05-21):** I have a port of this same patch running as a vendor edit inside the production OpenClaw container that services my Vietnamese customer-support bot (Em Huyền on Zalo personal). However, on careful review of the live gateway log I see `promptImages=0` on all 45 recent inbound photos despite the vendor patch being present. The patched `dosFetchInboundMedia` is not producing files under `/tmp/openclaw/` - meaning either the zca-js content shape for the user's photo does not pass my `extractInboundMedia` detection in production, or my path/permission setup is wrong.

The bot does answer correctly when the user sends a photo, but the path that works for me right now is the dynamic `image` tool round-trip (URL inlined in text -> bot calls `image` tool -> tool fetches + OCRs -> bot quotes result), not native vision via `buildContext({media: [...]})`.

Filing this PR as a **design proposal mirroring the Telegram/Discord pattern** rather than as "I verified the end-to-end vision path works in production". The shape of the code matches the patterns those extensions use and the unit tests pin the detection logic, but I cannot in good faith claim `promptImages=1` is reached on real Zalo CDN photos without a live trace showing that.

Two follow-ups I want to land before merge if you (steipete) are amenable:

1. Reproducible integration test in `zalouser` package that stubs zca-js with a photo content payload, runs `processMessage` against a real `core.channel.turn.buildContext` (or a faithful fixture), and asserts the resulting context carries the `media` facts.
2. Production-side instrumentation in DOS to debug why the vendor port produces `promptImages=0` and feed the fix back here.

Happy to iterate. If the design itself is wrong (e.g. `media` array on `buildContext` is not the canonical hook for native vision), I would rather find that out now and rework than ship a half-working path.

## Refs

- openclaw image tool design (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts` pattern)
- zca-js TAttachmentContent shape (`src/models/Message.ts`)

cc @steipete - sorry for the misleading first cut of this body; corrected now.
